headline: National Consultative Council on political financing met once then went silent
context: The National Consultative Council on Political Financing established in 2016 met once in December 2016 and has not convened since, leaving political financing completely unregulated.

cards.big: Political financing council met once in 2016 — then vanished for 10 years
cards.sub: Malaysia's only attempt at regulating political money died after a single meeting

cards.big: No legislation regulates political party financing, donations, or expenditure in Malaysia
cards.sub: Political parties can receive unlimited anonymous donations without disclosure

cards.big: Estimated RM2-4 billion flows through political parties annually with zero transparency
cards.sub: Unregulated political money is the foundation of patronage-based governance

cards.big: Indonesia enacted political financing law in 2017 — Malaysia still has nothing
cards.sub: Even regional neighbours with similar political cultures have moved ahead

cards.big: Unregulated political money doesn't buy democracy — it buys the people who run democracy
Should Malaysia enact mandatory political financing disclosure and spending caps?
